failure_surface: Initial code submissions contained XML-like wrapper tags (<QUESTION_SOLUTION>, <ANSWER>) embedded directly in executable code, causing Python SyntaxErrors. Despite eventually producing clean code that executed successfully, the test case evaluation returned 0, indicating formula or cell location misalignment.
root_cause: The agent confused multi-turn conversation framing markers with actual deliverable content. The solution code worked syntactically once tags were removed, but the persistent test failure (score 0) suggests either: (1) incorrect cell coordinates (row/column mapping), or (2) formula evaluation timing issues where TODAY() does not match expected test-case evaluation time.
minimal_fix_hypothesis: Agent should ensure output contains only raw executable code without any conversation markup tags, and validate cell references against worksheet schema before committing formula modifications.
evidence: Multiple SyntaxErrors in conversation_tail showing '<QUESTION_SOLUTION>from openpyxl...' parsing failures
SyntaxError pointed to line 1 indicating malformed content preceding import statements
Test case results show [0] despite '[Code executed successfully with no output]' in final attempt
Formula writes to ws.cell(row=5, column=5) targeting cell E5 per instruction
Dynamic TODAY() formula may produce different results than static test expectations

memory_items.title: Output Format Hygiene
memory_items.lesson: Executable code must not contain conversation markers, XML tags, or HTML wrappers that violate language syntax rules.
memory_items.generalization: Always strip non-code formatting artifacts before executing generated scripts; output should be pure syntactic code matching target language grammar.

memory_items.title: Cell Reference Verification
memory_items.lesson: When targeting specific cells via programmatic APIs, confirm row/column index semantics match the intended spreadsheet coordinates before writing.
memory_items.generalization: Validate cell positioning using worksheet headers or explicit naming conventions rather than assuming numeric row/column mappings without verification.

memory_items.title: Dynamic Formula Validation
memory_items.lesson: Formulas using volatile functions (TODAY(), NOW(), RAND()) will evaluate differently across test executions compared to static expected values.
memory_items.generalization: Prefer deterministic approaches when possible; if dynamic evaluation is required, ensure test infrastructure accounts for runtime variability or snapshot expected results appropriately.

edits.content:
## Response Formatting & Execution Integrity
- **Pure Code Output:** Generated Python code must never include conversation wrappers, XML tags, markdown blocks, or other artifacts that break language syntax. Strip all non-executable content before running.
- **Cell Coordinate Validation:** Verify row/column index semantics in openpyxl/writing libraries match spreadsheet layout expectations. Use named ranges or header lookups when possible instead of hardcoded positions.
- **Volatile Function Awareness:** Formulas containing TODAY(), NOW(), NOW(), or similar volatile functions evaluate at runtime and may not match pre-calculated test expectations. Design tests to accommodate dynamic evaluation or use constant values for reproducible validation.
